text:
----Security_Multipart(Mon_Dec_22_01_12_00_2008_252)--
Content-Type: Text/Plain; charset=us-ascii
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

Bengt Ahlgren <bengta@sics.se> wrote
  in <uh7vdtdfs3s.fsf@P142.sics.se>:

be> Hiroki Sato <hrs@FreeBSD.org> writes:
be>
be> > Bengt Ahlgren <bengta@sics.se> wrote
be> >   in <200812101814.mBAIERYg071437@www.freebsd.org>:
be> >
be> > be> The following error occurred while printing...
be> > be> '/libexec/ld-elf.so.1: /usr/local/Adobe/Reader8/ENU/Adobe/Reader8/Reader/intellinux/lib/libstdc++.so.6: unsupported file layout'
be> > be>
be> > be> and on amd64:
be> > be>
be> > be> The following error occurred while printing...
be> > be> '/libexec/ld-elf.so.1: Shared object "libm.so.6" not found, required by "libstdc++.so.6"'
be> > be>
be> > be> The reason is that the acroread launch script sets LD_LIBRARY_PATH to:
be> >
be> >  What is the helper program which occurs the above error exactly?
be>
be> KDE's kprinter program (which I entered as the print program in the
be> print dialogue).
be>
be> (But I think I got the error messages swapped amd64<->i386)

 So, can you try to create a simple wrapper script that removes
 LD_LIBRARY_PATH and exec kprinter, and then enter its filename in the
 print dialog?

--
| Hiroki SATO
